novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 | Conjectures 1-2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=field; the arbitrary-K rank gap and quotient certificate still read as a finite-algebra audit layer over Wang2026 process envelopes plus Winkler1988 moment geometry unless tied to a sharper inferential or identification theorem.

soundness_flags.label: Endpoint noninformation limit
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed Gamma=infinity CDF lower bound e_a(x)F_obs(y) cannot hold at the upper support endpoint, where any valid CDF must equal 1; the endpoint-closure convention needs to be stated explicitly.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Upgrade Conjecture 2 from a minimal-description certificate to a concrete simultaneous-inference or LP-complexity theorem showing a provable computational/statistical gain not recoverable from Wang2026 envelope bands.
State an explicit lower-bound theorem: any valid audit/inference procedure using only coordinatewise envelope or interval-hull summaries must fail on the Theorem 1 family for some Boolean CQTE claim.
Patch the Gamma=infinity sanity corollary with endpoint closure, e.g. restrict the displayed formula to interior thresholds or add the support-endpoint max/min convention.

recommended_next_step: Revise toward a field-level inferential or lower-bound payoff for the quotient rank, and patch the endpoint-limit corollary before rerunning novelty review.
